_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Translate (e.g., verbatim, gists, and/or summaries) target graphic material.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and relatable examples for non-technical business audiences without sacrificing accuracy.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-008 (DG, cosine: 0.373), DG-K-011 (DG, cosine: 0.361).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.525). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [gists, graphic, material, summaries, target, translate] vs WIDAI-only terms [accessible, accuracy, ai, audiences, business, concepts] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.827 (P=0.825, R=0.829). Balanced P/R — the 9-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Translate (e.g., verbatim, gists, and/or summaries) target graphic material." and "Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.377 < STS 0.525 (gap -0.148). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [gists, graphic, material] and [accessible, accuracy, ai] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [gists, graphic, material, summaries, target, translate]. WIDAI terms: [accessible, accuracy, ai, audiences, business, concepts].
